meta.title: HELIX align: detect drift in the artifact graph

narration: A team has a HELIX-governed project. The PRD says auth is API-key only, but a recent ADR proposes OAuth. Time to run alignment.

output:
docs/helix
├── 00-discover
│   └── product-vision.md
├── 01-frame
│   ├── concerns.md
│   ├── features
│   │   └── FEAT-001-auth.md
│   └── prd.md
└── 02-design
    └── adr
        └── ADR-002-oauth-pivot.md

narration: Now ask the agent to check alignment.

assistant: I'll walk the artifact graph in authority order — vision → PRD → features → ADRs — and look for drift.

output:
1. PRD R-3 (auth) specifies API-key only; ADR-002 adopts OAuth — contradiction at authority level.
  2. FEAT-001 still references API-key flow; design has shifted.
  3. No security-architecture artifact exists; OAuth introduces session/token lifecycle that needs one.

step 1  · revise PRD R-3 to allow OAuth (or rescope ADR-002)
  step 2  · author docs/helix/02-design/security-architecture.md
  step 3  · update FEAT-001-auth.md to track OAuth flow
  step 4  · add user stories US-OAUTH-{login,refresh,revoke}
  step 5  · revise test-plan to cover token lifecycle

narration: The plan is ordered by authority. The team reviews, approves, and hands it to the runtime. Alignment never invoked a CLI — only the agent and the skill.
